overview: Udyam Registration Portal is the official Government of India portal for MSME registration, launched on 1st July 2020 by the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises. It provides free, paperless, and digital registration for micro, small, and medium enterprises based on self-declaration of investment and turnover. The portal integrates with Income Tax and GST networks for automatic data fetching and issues a dynamic QR-coded certificate with lifetime validity.
objectives: Provide free and paperless MSME registration
Reduce transaction time and cost for entrepreneurs
Enable automatic data fetching from Income Tax and GST networks
Facilitate access to government schemes and priority sector lending
Support migration from Udyog Aadhaar to Udyam Registration
Allow registration of retail and wholesale trade activities under MSME for Priority Sector Lending
eligibility: Any person who intends to establish a micro, small or medium enterprise may file Udyam Registration online based on self-declaration with no requirement to upload documents, papers, certificates or proof. Classification is based on investment in plant and machinery or equipment and turnover: micro enterprise (investment ≤ ₹1 crore and turnover ≤ ₹5 crore), small enterprise (investment ≤ ₹10 crore and turnover ≤ ₹50 crore), medium enterprise (investment ≤ ₹50 crore and turnover ≤ ₹250 crore). All units with GSTIN listed against the same PAN are collectively treated as one enterprise.
benefits: Udyam Registration Certificate with dynamic QR code for verification, no renewal required, access to Priority Sector Lending from banks, eligibility for government subsidies and schemes, facilitation in government procurement via GeM integration, access to credit from financial institutions as per RBI notification, and availing of concessions and rebates under various central and state government schemes.
financial_support: Udyam Registration itself does not provide direct financial support but is mandatory to avail benefits under most Ministry of MSME schemes and to access credit from financial institutions as per RBI notification no. RBI/2020-2021/26 dated 21st August, 2020. The portal enables Priority Sector Lending for registered MSMEs.
application_process: 1. Visit the Udyam Registration Portal at https://udyamregistration.gov.in 2. Click on 'For New Enterprise who are not Registered yet as MSME' 3. Enter Aadhaar number and validate 4. Enter PAN details 5. Fill in enterprise details including name, type, address, bank details 6. Provide investment and turnover details (auto-fetched from IT/GST if available) 7. Submit self-declaration 8. Receive Udyam Registration Certificate with dynamic QR code
deadlines: Rolling basis — no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted year-round.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
caveats: No enterprise is supposed to file more than one Udyam Registration
Registration is based on self-declaration; false information may lead to penalties under MSMED Act, 2006
Benefits like Priority Sector Lending for retail and wholesale trade are restricted to that purpose only
Udyam Registration is mandatory to avail benefits under most Ministry of MSME schemes
